10.response: {"Q0":"Nothing was unclear to me and there were no issues during the experiment"}

6.response: {"Q0":"I only had one meteor shower after an astronaut left and I didn't collect their gem anymore because I knew I couldn't sell it to them"}

5.response: {"Q0":"No. Because I knew I couldn't sell that colour anymore and it would be a waste of my energy and the resources"}

0.response: {"Q0":"The only strategy I can think of is at the end when the Red Planet was no longer available to sell to. At the same time, I was getting unknown green gems so I completely stopped mining the planets for the last two rounds because there was no way to know what kind of gem I was getting and I couldn't sell half of them anymore so I figured I would just be wasting the gold trying to mine for a potential blue"}

7.response: {"Q0":"It made me not want to dig any planet anymore after the red astronaut left because it made every gem a potential red which I couldn't sell"}

9.response: {"Q0":"I concentrated more on picking the galaxies because the time limit was smaller and the planet mining became almost automatic"}

1.response: {"Q0":"It only became a strategy at the end and it didn't change until the game was over"}

2.response: {"Q0":"I found that I was more successful with the blue planet than the red. I got the blue maybe 60% of the time and the red about 30%"}

8.response: {"Q0":"I mostly picked the one with the better chance of winning unless it was a huge difference in reward, then I would pick the higher reward"}
